blocks.title: Ionic bond
blocks.rule: Rule: Δχ ≥ 1.7 or metal + nonmetal with high electronegativity difference
blocks.description:
Bond between a metal and a nonmetal by electron transfer. Ions are formed: cation (+) and anion (−).
Properties: High melting points, brittle, conducts electricity in melt and solution

blocks.title: Polar covalent bond
blocks.description:
Bond between different nonmetals via shared electron pairs. Electron density is shifted toward the more electronegative atom (δ+, δ−).
Properties: Various m.p., often liquids or gases, does not conduct electricity

blocks.title: Nonpolar covalent bond
blocks.rule: Rule: Δχ ≤ 0.4 (identical or very similar nonmetals)
blocks.description:
Bond between identical nonmetals via shared electron pairs. Electron density is distributed symmetrically.
Properties: Low m.p., gases or low-boiling liquids, does not conduct electricity

blocks.title: Metallic bond
blocks.rule: Rule: Pure metal or alloy (both metals)
blocks.description:
Bond in metals: atoms release valence electrons into a shared "electron cloud" that holds positive ions in the lattice.
Properties: Electrical and thermal conductivity, malleability, metallic luster

rows: Variable (−39 °C for Hg to 3422 °C for W)
